mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent within a single quarter, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples and drive negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal spending signals aggregate demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, depressing equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a steepening yield environment where short-term rates rise faster than long-term expectations, the cost of capital increases disproportionately for growth stocks, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS rises and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 percent annualized over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in housing starts reflects robust consumer confidence and construction activity, which historically correlates with positive equity market momentum over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST growth against 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in the industrial production index below 95.0 signals supply chain bottlenecks or capacity utilization stress that erodes profit margins and triggers negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of cases where INDPRO drops below the 95.0 threshold over a 4-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with a rising unemployment rate indicates a deteriorating labor market quality where job losses outpace new hires, signaling an impending earnings recession that drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating consumer price inflation, real disposable income growth turns negative, reducing retail spending capacity and corporate revenue growth, which suppresses equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 3.0 percent year-over-year and RSAFS declines over a 3-year rolling window.
